name: Telegram
description: Talk to this agent from Telegram — direct messages and group @mentions with inbound signature verification, replies, and typing indicators via eve's telegramChannel.
setup:
1. In Telegram, message **@BotFather**, create a bot, and copy its token into `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`.
2. Choose any hard-to-guess string for `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET_TOKEN` — eve uses it to verify inbound requests.
3. Deploy the agent. The Telegram route is `/eve/v1/telegram`; the full URL is `https://<your-domain>/e/<environmentId>/eve/v1/telegram`.
4. Register that URL with Telegram yourself — eve does **not** call `setWebhook` for you:

```
curl -X POST "https://api.telegram.org/bot$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/setWebhook" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url":"<your-endpoint-url>","secret_token":"<your-secret>","allowed_updates":["message","callback_query"]}'
```

To use the bot in group chats, set your bot's `@name` as `botUsername` in `channels/telegram.ts` and set privacy mode in BotFather. Telegram can't reach `localhost` — expose Eden with a tunnel in local development.

secrets.name: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
secrets.description: Bot API token from @BotFather — used for replies, typing indicators, callbacks, and proactive sends.

secrets.name: TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET_TOKEN
secrets.description: Secret you also register with setWebhook — eve checks it on the X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header of every inbound update.
